_meta.description: Canonical business command catalog derived from authored object packages with generator fallback.

commands.purpose: Archive a closed customer-care case under retention policy.
Close the case after the resolution is confirmed.
Escalate the case for higher-level attention.
Record service resolution against the case.
Assign and start active customer-care handling.
Pause the case pending customer response or evidence.
Acknowledge receipt of the customer PO before firm order confirmation.
Cancel the customer PO only while linked sales orders are not active.
Close the customer PO after all linked sales orders reach terminal states.
Confirm the customer PO only after at least one governed sales order link exists.
Record customer acceptance of the quotation.
Convert an accepted quote into governed sales-order demand.
Expire an unanswered quotation after validity lapse.
Record customer rejection of the quotation.
Reopen the quote for revision after response or expiry.
Release the quote to the customer after review.
Move the quote from draft into governed commercial review.
Cancel the sales order before final shipment closure.
Close the sales order after shipment completion and commercial closure.
Confirm the order after quote acceptance or customer PO linkage.
Move draft demand into quoted commercial offer posture.
Ship only after production and shipment gate controls pass.
Release confirmed order into production planning and JO/WO generation.
Close the invoice after payment or void is final.
Place invoice on governed hold for discrepancy resolution.
Complete three-way match for supplier invoice.
Post the matched invoice into the ledger.
Settle the AP invoice after payment execution.
Void the AP invoice before final close.
Close the AR invoice once settled or cancelled.
Post AR invoice into open receivable state.
Raise a governed customer dispute against the receivable.
Settle AR invoice after cash application.
Void or cancel the receivable before final close.
Close an exception after governed resolution or expiry handling.
Expire a timeboxed backdate exception once its window is no longer valid.
Revoke an approved backdate exception before it is used or after issue detection.
Create an approved credit memo with governed reference and reason.
Create an approved debit memo with governed reference and reason.
Approve and capitalize the governed asset value.
Post capitalization into asset and ledger truth.
Retire capitalized value from active service.
Void a prepared capitalization before posting.
Close or re-close a governed period after review.
Reopen a period under controlled exception handling.
Acknowledge and own the reconciliation exception for active triage.
Close the exception after reconciliation truth is restored.
Apply controlled compensating action and move exception to review.
Reject the current resolution path and return the issue for redesign or escalation.
Retry sync or replay after corrective action, pending review of effect.
Authorize the customer return before physical receipt.
Close the customer return after repair, replacement, or credit completion.
Disposition the return through credit issuance.
Start technical evaluation of the returned item.
Receive the returned item into controlled evaluation.
Disposition the return as repaired.
Disposition the return as replaced.
Close the plan once all count adjustments are resolved.
Complete the count plan after results and variances are recorded.
Pause a cycle-count plan under governed reason.
Release a cycle-count plan for active execution.
Book the carrier and lock the governed freight commitment.
Cancel the freight order before terminal execution.
Close the freight order after delivery reconciliation.
Record delivery confirmation and proof-of-delivery trace.
Start in-transit execution for the booked freight order.
Tender the freight requirement to controlled carrier selection.
Release the item for governed commercial, planning, and inventory use.
Block the item from new operational use without deleting history.
Retire the item from future operational use under controlled obsolescence.
Promote the item into controlled engineering release.
Cancel the movement before posting.
Hold the movement under reconciliation or quality concern.
Post the movement into governed inventory truth.
Reverse the posted movement under controlled correction.
Submit the movement for governed stock-effect review.
Cancel the shipment before final delivery.
Close the shipment after delivery confirmation and reconciliation.
Record proof of delivery.
Dispatch the shipment to logistics execution.
Prepare shipment after all release prerequisites are complete.
Release the shipment for dispatch.
Release the warehouse for governed receiving, stocking, and shipment use.
Archive the warehouse after operational retirement.
Place the warehouse on controlled hold.
Stop new operational use while retaining history.
Archive the closed 5S audit under retention policy.
Close the 5S audit after verification is complete.
Launch follow-up for findings and corrective ownership.
Start the 5S audit execution against the planned scope.
Verify that follow-up actions restored the expected 5S condition.
Archive a closed incident under retention policy.
Close the incident after corrective action or documented justification.
Record immediate containment against the incident.
Start formal incident investigation.
Move the incident into owned corrective action execution.
Triage a newly reported incident.
Activate the approved plan for governed scheduling and release.
Approve the preventive-maintenance plan for operational use.
Archive the retired maintenance plan under retention policy.
Hold the maintenance plan under deferral or equipment constraint.
Retire the maintenance plan from future release.
Cancel the maintenance work order before completion.
Complete maintenance execution with closeout trace.
Prepare the work order with governed maintenance scope.
Release the maintenance work order into execution.
Start maintenance execution on the governed work order.
Activate the permit for governed field execution.
Approve the permit with controlled conditions.
Archive the closed permit under retention policy.
Close the permit after work completion or controlled shutdown.
Submit the permit for governed approval.
Suspend the permit when conditions are no longer safe or valid.
Archive the closed observation under retention policy.
Assign the observation to a responsible owner.
Close the observation after action or controlled justification.
Start active containment or corrective response.
Archive a disposed asset under retention policy.
Dispose the asset with preserved financial and operational trace.
Place an asset on governed hold.
Retire the asset from active service.
Archive the closed obligation under retention policy.
Close the obligation after governed completion or waiver basis.
Mark the obligation as actively due for controlled follow-up.
Escalate the obligation into overdue state with management visibility.
Waive the obligation under documented approval.
Release the customer for governed commercial use.
Archive the inactive customer under retention policy.
Place the customer on controlled commercial hold.
Stop new commercial use while preserving historical trace.
Release the employee into governed operational scope.
Archive the terminated employee under retention policy.
Move the employee out of active assignment while preserving history.
Suspend the employee from active operational eligibility.
Terminate the employee under governed HR control.
Release qualified equipment into active operational use.
Archive retired equipment under retention policy.
Place equipment on hold under governed operational restriction.
Apply lockout to prevent unsafe operational use.
Qualify the equipment for governed operational use.
Retire the equipment from future maintenance and execution release.
Archive the blocked supplier under retention policy.
Authorize governed spend with the qualified supplier.
Block the supplier from new procurement release.
Place the supplier on controlled hold.
Qualify the supplier for controlled sourcing use.
Archive the retired tool under retention policy.
Issue the tool into active operational use.
Block the tool after breakage or unsafe condition is detected.
Mark the tool as worn based on usage or inspection.
Release a new or restored tool into governed issue readiness.
Retire the tool from further issue or use.
Record operator acknowledgement against a machine-originated alarm.
Close the alarm response while preserving machine source, operator, and downtime evidence.
Escalate an alarm to supervisor, maintenance, or quality when it affects production or safety.
Archive the closed or cancelled dispatch entry.
Cancel the dispatch entry before terminal execution.
Close the dispatch entry after completion reconciliation.
Complete the dispatch execution after governed shopfloor confirmation.
Place the dispatch entry on hold under controlled reason.
Release the dispatch entry into governed shopfloor execution.
Start execution against the released dispatch entry.
Accept the in-process check after evidence review.
Close the IPQC record after acceptance or managed rejection.
Send the inspection back for reinspection after corrective action.
Reject the in-process check and trigger containment.
Start in-process inspection execution.
Cancel the job order before irreversible downstream execution.
Close the job order after completion and reconciliation.
Mark the job order execution complete before administrative close.
Put the job order on hold for shortage, engineering, or quality reasons.
Release the job order for executable downstream work preparation.
Move the job order into active execution supervision.
Cancel the operation before execution begins.
Complete the production operation with governed execution totals.
Hold the operation under quality concern.
Declare the operation ready for governed execution.
Record scrap against the production operation.
Start the production operation.
Approve the reviewed production plan.
Archive a terminal production plan under retention policy.
Complete the plan after execution reconciliation.
Freeze the published plan under controlled schedule discipline.
Put the plan on hold pending decision or disruption handling.
Publish the approved plan into dispatch and execution truth.
Submit the production plan for review.
Cancel a non-started or non-terminal work order.
Close execution after production trace is complete.
Dispatch the work order to the shopfloor.
Pause execution under a controlled quality hold.
Resume execution after quality hold resolution.
Start shopfloor execution.
Accept received material into governed pass state.
Queue an incoming inspection against received material.
Reject material and trigger quarantine or NCR escalation.
Start the IQC execution while remaining in pending review.
Waive full acceptance with controlled conditional disposition.
Approve the correction after inventory, supplier, and AP impact are reviewed.
Post the correction into stock and financial truth.
Submit a governed receipt correction request for review.
Void a correction before posting or final reconciliation.
Accept received material after IQC completion.
Close the receipt after physical and financial completion.
Put away accepted material into controlled stock.
Quarantine receipt pending disposition.
Reverse a receipt under controlled correction rules.
Move received material into governed IQC review.
Approve requisition demand for sourcing.
Cancel the requisition before sourcing finishes.
Mark the requisition as sourced into supplier execution.
Submit a requisition for approval.
Cancel an ASN before physical receipt enters stock truth.
Close the ASN after receipt and discrepancy handling are complete.
Confirm the supplier ASN against the governed PO and expected inbound plan.
Record ASN discrepancy against PO, packing, or physical inbound condition.
Mark that the ASN quantity physically arrived for receipt creation.
Move the ASN into active receiving execution.
Approve supplier PO for supplier commitment.
Close supplier PO after governed receiving completion.
Record full receipt against the released supplier PO.
Record partial receipt against the released supplier PO.
Release the PO to the supplier after approval.
Submit supplier PO for governed approval.
Approve the audit plan and finding criteria.
Close the audit after findings and follow-up are complete.
Issue findings and track governed responses.
Reject the audit package or closure path under governed reason.
Schedule the audit with governed scope and ownership.
Start audit execution and evidence capture.
Verify that findings were effectively addressed.
Approve and close the concession with explicit evidence.
Reject the concession under governed reason.
Start governed review of the concession request.
Move the concession into approval review.
Activate a reviewed control plan for production or inspection use.
Archive a superseded control plan while preserving revision and approval evidence.
Revise control-plan content without deleting the approved historical record.
Close the CAPA after effectiveness is verified.
Execute approved corrective actions.
Initiate CAPA from NCR, audit, or systemic signal.
Record owned CAPA tasks and deadlines.
Run effectiveness review against implemented action.
Close the deviation after approved disposition and evidence review.
Reject the deviation path under governed reason.
Start governed investigation or mitigation for the deviation.
Move the deviation into approval review.
Approve the improvement for execution.
Archive a closed or rejected improvement under retention policy.
Close the improvement after verification and benefit confirmation.
Pause the improvement with governed reason and recovery plan.
Execute the approved countermeasure or kaizen action.
Reject an improvement that does not meet value or readiness criteria.
Start governance review for feasibility and value impact.
Submit an improvement proposal with problem statement and target KPI.
Verify realized benefit or learning after implementation.
Escalate unacceptable GRR or NDC result to calibration, NCR or improvement action.
Record a new MSA/GRR study with device, operator, sample, trial and result evidence.
Append or correct MSA evidence without overwriting the original audit trail.
Activate containment against affected stock, WIP, or shipment.
Close the NCR after all required evidence and linked actions are complete.
Request closure after containment and disposition evidence are complete.
Start formal quality review.
Record the approved disposition path.
Submit the nonconformance for formal review.
Accept the outgoing lot or unit for shipment release.
Reject the outgoing inspection and block shipment release.
Start the outgoing inspection while maintaining pending status.
Waive the inspection under controlled approval.
Close the override after follow-up and exception handling are completed.
Expire an active override at the governed end of its validity window.
Revoke an active override before the exception remains valid.
Escalate an out-of-control or capability breach into NCR/CAPA follow-up.
Evaluate SPC rules and capability signals against the recorded sample stream.
Record a governed SPC sample with process, job, operator, timestamp and measurement context.
Correct genealogy evidence by supersession or annotated correction without deleting the original trace.
Record a parent-child genealogy link from production, receiving, or serialization evidence.
